title: strychnos myrtoides
intro: Strychnos myrtoides, also known as the West African poison nut or poison wood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Loganiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Strychnos myrtoides include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate joint pain, and treat fungal infections. Herbal preparations of Strychnos myrtoides include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
